entry.title: [기업 약속 diff — AI 사용정책·원칙·안전 프레임워크] [변경] commitment:microsoft:responsible-ai · text
entry.content: {"after":"Responsible AI Principles and Approach | Microsoft AI This is the Trace Id: ceb383daf28fe3fefdd21f4673c9596e Skip to main content Microsoft AI Frontier Transformation Frontier Accelerate AI for business Use cases Consumer goods Digital sovereignty Education Overview Power and utilities Oil and gas Mining Overview Banking Capital markets Insurance Overview Defense and intelligence Transportation and urban infrastructure Social services and public health Public safety and justice Public finance Overview Defense and intelligence Federal civilian State and local governments Cloud for US government AI for US government Overview Providers Payors Life sciences Health solutions Overview Industrial transformation Media and entertainment Overview Automotive Travel and transportation Retail Telecommunications Microsoft 365 Copilot AI agents at work Microsoft IQ Agent 365 Security for AI Copilot Studio Microsoft Foundry Microsoft Agent Factory Azure AI apps and agents Microsoft Marketplace Copilot+ PCs Microsoft Copilot Download the Copilot app Microsoft responsible AI Principles and approach Tools and practices Advancing sustainability Securing AI Data protection and privacy AI 101 AI learning hub Industry blog Microsoft Cloud blog Support for business Industry documentation Microsoft Responsible AI Principles and approach \"> Principles and approach We're committed to developing AI systems in a way that is transparent, reliable, and worthy of trust. AI principles AI principles Responsible AI Standard Implementing responsible AI Transparency report Transparency documents FAQ Principles Defining what’s important \"> Defining what’s important We've identified six principles that we believe should guide AI development and use. Fairness AI systems should treat all people fairly. How might an AI system allocate opportunities, resources, and information in ways that are fair to the humans who use it? See fairness in action Reliability and safety AI systems should perform reliably and safely. How might a system function well for people across different use conditions and contexts, including ones that it wasn’t originally intended for? See reliability and safety in action Privacy and security AI systems should be secure and respect privacy. How might a system be designed to support privacy and security? See privacy and security in action Inclusiveness AI systems should empower everyone and engage all people, regardless of their backgrounds. How might a system be designed to be inclusive for people of all abilities? See inclusiveness in action Transparency AI systems should be understandable. How can we ensure that people correctly understand the capabilities of a system? See transparency in action Accountability People should be accountable for AI systems. How can we create oversight so that humans can be accountable and in control? See accountability in action Fairness Previous Slide Next Slide News Measurement is the key to helping keep AI on track Read more Research Maximizing benefits and minimizing harms with language technologies Read more Research How AI teams adapt fairness checklists to real-world work Read more Research Fairness, accountability, transparency, and ethics in AI Read more Blog Microsoft’s responsible AI principles for accessible technology Read more Tool Fairlearn Python package on GitHub Read more Podcast Measuring and mitigating potential AI harms Read more Back to Responsible AI in action - Fairness tab section Reliability and safety Previous Slide Next Slide News Why AI sometimes gets it wrong—and big strides to address it Read more Blog Evaluating and understanding progress in AI Read more Blog Reimagining the foundation of agentic AI for scale, extensibility, and robustness Read more Blog Microsoft AI safety policies updated for the UK AI Safety Summit Read more Blog Microsoft joins nonprofits to enact child safety commitments for generative AI Read more Blog Generative AI highlights the importance of family conversations about online safety Read more Blog Disrupting a global cybercrime network abusing generative AI Read more Blog Tackling abusive AI-generated content risks through education and empowerment Read more Blog Innovating in line with the European Union’s AI Act Read more Videos Azure AI Content Safety demos and quick tutorials for developers Watch the videos GitHub Explore sample code and assets for Azure AI Content Safety training Find Examples Back to Responsible AI in action - Reliability and safety tab section Privacy and security Previous Slide Next Slide News Safeguarding AI against “jailbreaks” and other prompt attacks Read more News Red teams think like hackers to help keep AI safe Read more Blog Technical deep dive into Azure AI confidential inferencing Read more Research Reducing data leakage risks in AI models Read more Blog Expanding AccountGuard to counter growing cyberthreats in Africa Read more Blog Enhancing trust and protecting privacy in the AI era Read more Blog Protecting the data of our commercial and public sector customers in the AI era Read more Talk Ensuring privacy in AI with homomorphic encryption Watch the video Project Confidential AI from Microsoft Read more Blog How Microsoft protects AI platforms against cyberthreats Learn more Back to Responsible AI in action - Privacy and security tab section Inclusiveness Previous Slide Next Slide Video Our Agentic Era: Building Accessible and Responsible AI for Everyone Watch now Research Accelerating multilingual Retrieval-Augmented Generation systems Read more News Stories of AI impact in 2024 Read more Blog Improving disability representation to drive AI innovation Read more Blog The technology, people, partnerships, and policies driving a more accessible future Read more Blog Microsoft collaborates with partners and policymakers to advance accessibility Read more Blog Microsoft’s pledge to the National AI Research Resource pilot Read more Blog Six ways to improve accessibility with Azure AI Read more Back to Responsible AI in action - Inclusiveness tab section Transparency Previous Slide Next Slide News Fighting deepfakes with more transparency about AI Read more Report Developing safe, secure, and trustworthy AI Read more Blog Providing further transparency on our responsible AI efforts Read more Book chapter Using a human-centered agenda for intelligible machine learning Read more Publication Enhancing transparency and accountability in AI with datasheets for datasets Read more Back to Responsible AI in action - Transparency tab section Accountability Previous Slide Next Slide Video Foundry Control Plane: Managing AI agents at scale Watch now News Making it easier for companies to build and ship AI people can trust Read more Blog Taking legal action to protect the public from abusive AI-generated content Read more Blog Disrupting a global cybercrime network abusing generative AI Read more Forum Redefining AI with in-house models and transparency Read more Blog Announcing Microsoft’s AI Customer Commitments Read more Back to Responsible AI in action - Accountability tab section The Microsoft Responsible AI Standard \"> The Microsoft Responsible AI Standard Explore guidance from Microsoft on how to design, build, and test AI systems. Get the Standard Reference guide How Microsoft implements responsible AI \"> How Microsoft implements responsible AI Explore how Microsoft empowers employees across the organization to be champions of responsible AI. Policy Research Engineering Previous Next Governance We set rules for enacting responsible AI and clearly define roles and responsibilities for teams involved. Team enablement We foster readiness to adopt responsible AI practices, both within our company and with our customers and partners. Sensitive use We review sensitive use cases to ensure we are upholding our responsible AI principles. Public policy We work to shape new laws and standards to help ensure that the promise of AI is realized for society at large. Overview Researchers in Aether, Microsoft Research, and our engineering teams keep our Responsible AI Standard on the leading edge. AI research Teams conduct rigorous AI research, including on transparency, fairness, human-AI collaboration, privacy, security, safety and the impact of AI on people and society. Perspectives Our researchers actively participate in broader discussions and debates to ensure that our responsible AI program integrates big-picture perspectives and input. Building responsible AI tooling and systems Engineering teams define and operationalize a tooling and system strategy for using AI responsibly. Engineering practices Engineering leaders identify and implement engineering practices that integrate responsible AI into everyday work. Compliance mechanisms Engineering teams implement compliance tooling to help monitor and enforce responsible AI rules and requirements. Back to tabs SECURITY AND COMPLIANCE The Microsoft 2025 Responsible AI Transparency Report \"> The Microsoft 2025 Responsible AI Transparency Report Our annual responsible AI report reflects a commitment to openness and ethical AI development. Explore the 2025 report to learn how Microsoft: Builds generative AI systems responsibly. Makes decisions about releasing these systems. Helps customers build AI responsibly. Evolves our responsible AI program. Get the 2025 report Get the 2024 report Transparency documents Transparency documents \"> Transparency documents Understand how our AI technology works, so you can create a system that fits your needs. Anomaly Detector \"> Anomaly Detector Analyze time series data using timestamps and numerical metrics to receive the anomalous status of each data point along with contributing variables. Read more Azure AI Content Safety \"> Azure AI Content Safety Detect harmful content in apps and services using text, image, and multimodal APIs—plus, use custom categories for tailored content moderation needs. Read more Azure AI Face service \"> Azure AI Face service Enhance efficiency, security, and customer experiences with systems that analyze people’s faces. Read more Azure AI Search \"> Azure AI Search Get AI-enhanced tools, APIs, and SDKs for building rich search experiences for diverse content in web, mobile, and enterprise apps. Read more Azure Language in Foundry Tools \"> Azure Language in Foundry Tools Azure AI Language in Foundry Tools is a cloud-based service that provides Natural Language Processing (NLP) features for text mining and text analysis. Read more Azure OpenAI \"> Azure OpenAI Use OpenAI models to generate natural language, code, and images. Integrated content filtering and abuse detection is included. Read more Azure Video Indexer \"> Azure Video Indexer Analyze video and audio files with AI to generate insights like detected objects, faces, and transcriptions in more than 60 languages. Read more Click to Do \"> Click to Do A Windows feature that provides intelligent shortcuts to consumers so they can quickly complete a task based on what they are viewing. Read more Load more Frequently asked questions Expand all Collapse all 01/ What are AI principles and why are they important? AI principles are guidelines designed to ensure the responsible development and deployment of AI technologies. These principles are crucial because they help mitigate risks, promote ethical practices, and maximize the benefits of AI for society. The Responsible AI Standard at Microsoft consolidates essential practices to ensure compliance with emerging AI laws and regulations. 02/ Are there tools available to help my organization implement responsible AI practices? Microsoft offers a range of tools and practices to help organizations practice responsible AI. Additionally, the Responsible AI Standard at Microsoft helps define product development requirements for responsible AI. 03/ What are Transparency Notes and how do I use them? Transparency Notes are created to help customers better understand the inner workings of AI technologies and make more informed decisions about their use. They are part of the Responsible AI Standard and are intended to support responsible AI development by providing insights into how AI systems are governed, mapped, measured, and managed. Microsoft also offers the Responsible AI Transparency Report , which provides insights into how Microsoft builds apps with generative AI, oversees the deployment of those apps, supports customers as they build their own AI apps, and fosters a responsible AI community. Discover more \"> Discover more Tools and practices Get tools to support responsible AI practices Explore tools AI policy and regulation Discover the latest perspectives on AI policy from Microsoft experts Explore policies Accelerate business growth with trustworthy AI Learn how to accelerate AI adoption, reduce risk, and strengthen customer confidence by using AI responsibly and securely. Watch the webinar Follow Microsoft Anomaly Detector \"> Anomaly Detector Analyze time series data using timestamps and numerical metrics to receive the anomalous status of each data point along with contributing variables. Read more Azure AI Content Safety \"> Azure AI Content Safety Detect harmful content in apps and services using text, image, and multimodal APIs—plus, use custom categories for tailored content moderation needs. Read more Azure AI Face service \"> Azure AI Face service Enhance efficiency, security, and customer experiences with systems that analyze people’s faces. Read more Azure AI Search \"> Azure AI Search Get AI-enhanced tools, APIs, and SDKs for building rich search experiences for diverse content in web, mobile, and enterprise apps. Read more Azure Language in Foundry Tools \"> Azure Language in Foundry Tools Azure AI Language in Foundry Tools is a cloud-based service that provides Natural Language Processing (NLP) features for text mining and text analysis. Read more Azure OpenAI \"> Azure OpenAI Use OpenAI models to generate natural language, code, and images. Integrated content filtering and abuse detection is included. Read more Azure Video Indexer \"> Azure Video Indexer Analyze video and audio files with AI to generate insights like detected objects, faces, and transcriptions in more than 60 languages. Read more Click to Do \"> Click to Do A Windows feature that provides intelligent shortcuts to consumers so they can quickly complete a task based on what they are viewing. Read more Conversational language understanding \"> Conversational language understanding Interpret natural language text so client apps, like chatbots, can understand and act on user requests. Read more Custom named entity recognition \"> Custom named entity recognition Build custom models for extracting domain-specific entities from unstructured text. Read more Custom text classification \"> Custom text classification Build custom models for text classification tasks that support both single and multiple label classifications. Read more Custom Vision \"> Custom Vision Classify and detect objects in images based on visual characteristics, so you can build, deploy, and improve your image identification system. Read more Image Analysis \"> Image Analysis Process media assets to extract visual features, like objects and people, for knowledge mining, automation, and accessibility. Read more Key Phrase Extraction \"> Key Phrase Extraction Quickly identify main concepts in text. You’ll receive key points like "food" and "wonderful staff" while discarding nonessential words. Read more Language Detection \"> Language Detection Across multiple languages and dialects, identify the input text language, and get a single language code with a confidence score for each document. Read more Language Understanding \"> Language Understanding Interpret natural language text, predict overall meaning, and extract specific information for client apps, like chatbots. Read more Microsoft 365 Copilot \"> Microsoft 365 Copilot An AI-powered productivity tool that helps enhance your creativity, productivity, and skills by using large language models (LLMs) and content that you have access to. Read more Microsoft Copilot Studio \"> Microsoft Copilot Studio Copilot Studio is a platform that empowers organizations to build, customize, and deploy AI-driven agents to automate tasks, answer questions, and streamline processes. Read more Microsoft Foundry on Windows – Image AI APIs \"> Microsoft Foundry on Windows – Image AI APIs On-device Image AI APIs and capabilities that enable developers to analyze, modify, and enhance images. Read more Microsoft Foundry on Windows – Phi Silica (Language Model) \"> Microsoft Foundry on Windows – Phi Silica (Language Model) A small, efficient language model designed to run directly on a Windows PC's Neural Processing Unit (NPU). Read more Microsoft Foundry on Windows – Text Recognition (OCR) \"> Microsoft Foundry on Windows – Text Recognition (OCR) Enables applications to extract machine-readable text from images and documents directly on a Windows device. Read more Microsoft Paint \"> Microsoft Paint A built-in Windows application that enables users to create, edit, and modify digital images. Read more Optical character recognition \"> Optical character recognition Extract text from images and documents in multiple languages. Handwritten text is also supported in English. Read more Personalizer \"> Personalizer Use reinforcement learning to help apps choose the best content for users—it continuously improves based on their reactions and contextual information. Read more  Personally identifiable information (PII) \"> Personally identifiable information (PII) Identify and categorize personal entities, including PII and protected health information, from text. Read more Pronunciation Assessment \"> Pronunciation Assessment Evaluate speech pronunciation in multiple languages for feedback on accuracy, fluency, vocabulary, grammar, and topic understanding. Read more QnA Maker \"> QnA Maker Create a conversational layer over your data using Azure resources to find the best answer for natural language inputs from a custom knowledge base. Read more Question answering \"> Question answering Build conversational apps, like social media apps, chatbots, and speech-enabled desktop apps, that communicate with users in natural language. Read more Recall \"> Recall Allows users to search locally saved and locally analyzed snapshots of their screen using natural language. Read more Sentiment Analysis \"> Sentiment Analysis Evaluate text to detect positive, neutral, and negative sentiments. You’ll receive scores and labels for each sentence in various scenarios. Read more Speech to text \"> Speech to text Convert spoken audio into text to support more than 140 locales (part of Azure AI services). Read more Summarization \"> Summarization Condense articles, papers, and documents into key takeaways, so developers can build intelligent solutions. Read more Text Analytics for health \"> Text Analytics for health Find and label health information in unstructured text to support customization and multiple languages. Read more Text to speech \"> Text to speech Convert written text into high-quality speech audio while supporting prebuilt, custom neural voices and offering visual capabilities with synthetic video avatars. Read more Trial Matcher \"> Trial Matcher Match patients to suitable clinical trials based on unique characteristics and trial criteria. Read more Windows Studio Effects \"> Windows Studio Effects This dedicated hardware accelerator enables real-time enhancements to video calls and recordings. sustainability Securing AI Data protection and privacy AI 101 AI learning hub Industry blog Microsoft Cloud blog Support for business Industry documentation Microsoft Responsible AI Principles and approach \"> Principles and approach We're committed to developing AI systems in a way that is transparent, reliable, and worthy of trust. AI principles AI principles Responsible AI Standard Implementing responsible AI Transparency report Transparency documents FAQ Principles Defining what’s important \"> Defining what’s important We've identified six principles that we believe should guide AI development and use. Fairness AI systems should treat all people fairly. How might an AI system allocate opportunities, resources, and information in ways that are fair to the humans who use it? See fairness in action Reliability and safety AI systems should perform reliably and safely. How might a system function well for people across different use conditions and contexts, including ones that it wasn’t originally intended for? See reliability and safety in action Privacy and security AI systems should be secure and respect privacy. How might a system be designed to support privacy and security? See privacy and security in action Inclusiveness AI systems should empower everyone and engage all people, regardless of their backgrounds. How might a system be designed to be inclusive for people of all abilities? See inclusiveness in action Transparency AI systems should be understandable. How can we ensure that people correctly understand the capabilities of a system? See transparency in action Accountability People should be accountable for AI systems. How can we create oversight so that humans can be accountable and in control? Accountability tab section The Microsoft Responsible AI Standard \"> The Microsoft Responsible AI Standard Explore guidance from Microsoft on how to design, build, and test AI systems. Get the Standard Reference guide How Microsoft implements responsible AI \"> How Microsoft implements responsible AI Explore how Microsoft empowers employees across the organization to be champions of responsible AI. Policy Research Engineering Previous Next Governance We set rules for enacting responsible AI and clearly define roles and responsibilities for teams involved. Team enablement We foster readiness to adopt responsible AI practices, both within our company and with our customers and partners. Sensitive use We review sensitive use cases to ensure we are upholding our responsible AI principles. Public policy We work to shape new laws and standards to help ensure that the promise of AI is realized for society at large. Overview Researchers in Aether, Microsoft Research, and our engineering teams keep our Responsible AI Standard on the leading edge. AI research Teams conduct rigorous AI research, including on transparency, fairness, human-AI collaboration, privacy, security, safety and the impact of AI on people and society. Perspectives Our researchers actively participate in broader discussions and debates to ensure that our responsible AI program integrates big-picture perspectives and input. Building responsible AI tooling and systems Engineering teams define and operationalize a tooling and system strategy for using AI responsibly. Engineering practices Engineering leaders identify and implement engineering practices that integrate responsible AI into everyday work. Compliance mechanisms Engineering teams implement compliance tooling to help monitor and enforce responsible AI rules and requirements. Back to tabs SECURITY AND COMPLIANCE The Microsoft 2025 Responsible AI Transparency Report \"> The Microsoft 2025 Responsible AI Transparency Report Our annual responsible AI report reflects a commitment to openness and ethical AI development. Explore the 2025 report to learn how Microsoft: Builds generative AI systems responsibly. Makes decisions about releasing these systems. Helps customers build AI responsibly. Evolves our responsible AI program. Get the 2025 report Get the 2024 report Transparency documents Transparency documents \"> Transparency documents Understand how our AI technology works, so you can create a system that fits your needs. Read more Load more Frequently asked questions Expand all Collapse all 01/ What are AI principles and why are they important? AI principles are guidelines designed to ensure the responsible development and deployment of AI technologies. These principles are crucial because they help mitigate risks, promote ethical practices, and maximize the benefits of AI for society. The Responsible AI Standard at Microsoft consolidates essential practices to ensure compliance with emerging AI laws and regulations. 02/ Are there tools available to help my organization implement responsible AI practices? Microsoft offers a range of tools and practices to help organizations practice responsible AI. Additionally, the Responsible AI Standard at Microsoft helps define product development requirements for responsible AI. 03/ What are Transparency Notes and how do I use them? Transparency Notes are created to help customers better understand the inner workings of AI technologies and make more informed decisions about their use. They are part of the Responsible AI Standard and are intended to support responsible AI development by providing insights into how AI systems are governed, mapped, measured, and managed. Microsoft also offers the Responsible AI Transparency Report , which provides insights into how Microsoft builds apps with generative AI, oversees the deployment of those apps, supports customers as they build their own AI apps, and fosters a responsible AI community. Trustworthy AI Overview Trustworthy AI Solutions and Partners AI News Discover AI at GTC Overview Trustworthy AI Solutions and Partners AI News Discover AI at GTC Overview Trustworthy AI Solutions and Partners AI News Discover AI at GTC We believe AI should respect privacy and data protection regulations, operate in a secure and safe way, function in a transparent and accountable manner, and avoid unwanted biases and discrimination. We are committed to safe and trustworthy AI, in line with the White House Voluntary Commitments and other global AI Safety initiatives. Our Guiding Principles for Trustworthy AI Privacy AI should comply with privacy laws and regulations, and meet societal norms for personal data and information privacy. Safety and Security Ensure that AI systems perform as intended and avoid unintended harm and malicious threats. Transparency Make AI technology understandable to people. Explain, in non-technical language, how an AI system arrived at its output. Nondiscrimination Minimize bias in our AI systems and give all groups an equal opportunity to benefit from AI. Methods and Technologies Trustworthy AI principles are foundational to our end-to-end development and essential for the technical excellence that enables partners, customers, and developers to do their best work. We are building data factories for generative AI services , tools to curate and validate unbiased datasets for computer vision , libraries for scaling natural language processing data for large language models , groundbreaking security features like confidential computing , and innovations like open-source techniques for model alignment with human feedback. To check out the free, open source templates NVIDIA has provided to support the development of transparent AI documentation, visit the NVIDIA/Trustworthy-AI GitHub repo . With these expanded resources, NVIDIA is making it easier for customers and partners to responsibly develop AI and advance transparency and accountability across the AI supply chain. Trustworthiness is a fundamental property of our technology. — Jensen Huang, NVIDIA founder and CEO Quote 1 Our Trustworthy AI Solutions Model Card Generator AI model cards are the standard for increasing confidence in the development lifecycle, demonstrating compliance, and encouraging transparency. Minimize manual effort and increase efficiency by automating the creation of model cards. Enhance AI Model Transparency With Minimal Effort NVIDIA Halos for AI Safety NVIDIA Halos is a full-stack, comprehensive safety stack that unifies architecture, AI models, chips, software, tools, and services to ensure the safe development of physical AI like autonomous vehicles and robotics. Learn How NVIDIA Is Building Autonomous Vehicle Safety NeMo Guardrails NVIDIA NeMo Guardrails helps ensure that smart applications powered by large language models (LLM) are accurate, appropriate, on topic, and secure. Learn More About NVIDIA NeMo Guardrails Partnering for Trustworthy AI Technology Te Hiku Media NVIDIA Inception member Te Hiku Media created a highly accurate bilingual speech recognition system for the Māori language and NZ English, built and owned by its own language community. Learn More About Te Hiku Media Google DeepMind’s Synth ID NVIDIA is the first external user to embed digital watermarks directly into AI-generated audio, images, text, and video content to safeguard against misinformation and misattribution without compromising video quality. Learn More About NVIDIA and Google’s Work to Build Trust SIGNS NVIDIA is partnering with the American Society for Deaf Children, Hello Monday/DEPT, and the Rochester Institute for Technology to teach and build a publicly available dataset for accessible technologies through the Signs Platform. Learn and Help Build the World’s Largest Dataset for American Sign Language Trustworthy AI in the News View All Trustworthy AI Stories Corporate Blog Technical Blog A Commitment to Research Our AI research is focused on developing algorithms and systems that can augment human capabilities, solve complex problems, and improve efficiencies across industries. We work to maintain our guiding principles of privacy, transparency, nondiscrimination, and safety and security in all research practices and methodologies. Featured publications and papers: A Safety and Security Framework for Real-World Agentic Systems Shaona Ghosh, Barnaby Simkin, Dan Zhao, Nikki Pope, Roopa Prabhu, Michael Demoret, Bartley Richardson, et al. advancement of scientific innovations with QPUs Robotics and Edge AI Overview Innovative solutions to take on robotics, edge, and vision AI challenges Robotics GPU-accelerated advances in AI perception, simulation, and software Edge AI Bring the power of NVIDIA AI to the edge for real-time decision-making solutions Vision AI Transform data into valuable insights using vision AI AI Grid Scale AI-native services efficiently across connected, distributed AI infrastructure Autonomous Vehicles Overview AI-enhanced vehicles are transforming the future of mobility Open Source AV Models and Tools For reasoning-based AV systems AV Simulation Explore high-fidelity sensor simulation for safe autonomous vehicle development Reference Architecture Enables vehicles to be L4-ready Infrastructure Essential data center tools for safe autonomous vehicle development In-Vehicle Computing Develop automated driving functions and immersive in-cabin experiences Safety State-of-the-art system for AV safety, from the cloud to the car Industries Overview Architecture, Engineering, Construction & Operations Automotive Energy Financial Services Government Healthcare and Life Sciences Higher Education Industrial Sector Media and Entertainment Restaurants Retail and CPG Robotics Semiconductor Telecommunications Shop Drivers Support AI Trust Center --> This site requires Javascript in order to view all its content. Please enable Javascript in order to access all the functionality of this web site. Here are the instructions how to enable JavaScript in your web browser. Trustworthy AI Overview Trustworthy AI Solutions and Partners AI News Discover AI at GTC Overview Trustworthy AI Solutions and Partners AI News Discover AI at GTC Overview Trustworthy AI Solutions and Partners AI News Discover AI at GTC We believe AI should respect privacy and data protection regulations, operate in a secure and safe way, function in a transparent and accountable manner, and avoid unwanted biases and discrimination. We are committed to safe and trustworthy AI, in line with the White House Voluntary Commitments and other global AI Safety initiatives. Our Guiding Principles for Trustworthy AI Privacy AI should comply with privacy laws and regulations, and meet societal norms for personal data and information privacy. Safety and Security Ensure that AI systems perform as intended and avoid unintended harm and malicious threats. Transparency Make AI technology understandable to people. Explain, in non-technical language, how an AI system arrived at its output. Nondiscrimination Minimize bias in our AI systems and give all groups an equal opportunity to benefit from AI. Methods and Technologies Trustworthy AI principles are foundational to our end-to-end development and essential for the technical excellence that enables partners, customers, and developers to do their best work. We are building data factories for generative AI services , tools to curate and validate unbiased datasets for computer vision , libraries for scaling natural language processing data for large language models , groundbreaking security features like confidential computing , and innovations like open-source techniques for model alignment with human feedback. To check out the free, open source templates NVIDIA has provided to support the development of transparent AI documentation, visit the NVIDIA/Trustworthy-AI GitHub repo . With these expanded resources, NVIDIA is making it easier for customers and partners to responsibly develop AI and advance transparency and accountability across the AI supply chain. Trustworthiness is a fundamental property of our technology. — Jensen Huang, NVIDIA founder and CEO Quote 1 Our Trustworthy AI Solutions Model Card Generator AI model cards are the standard for increasing confidence in the development lifecycle, demonstrating compliance, and encouraging transparency. Minimize manual effort and increase efficiency by automating the creation of model cards. Enhance AI Model Transparency With Minimal Effort NVIDIA Halos for AI Safety NVIDIA Halos is a full-stack, comprehensive safety stack that unifies architecture, AI models, chips, software, tools, and services to ensure the safe development of physical AI like autonomous vehicles and robotics. Learn How NVIDIA Is Building Autonomous Vehicle Safety NeMo Guardrails NVIDIA NeMo Guardrails helps ensure that smart applications powered by large language models (LLM) are accurate, appropriate, on topic, and secure. Learn More About NVIDIA NeMo Guardrails Partnering for Trustworthy AI Technology Te Hiku Media NVIDIA Inception member Te Hiku Media created a highly accurate bilingual speech recognition system for the Māori language and NZ English, built and owned by its own language community. Learn More About Te Hiku Media Google DeepMind’s Synth ID NVIDIA is the first external user to embed digital watermarks directly into AI-generated audio, images, text, and video content to safeguard against misinformation and misattribution without compromising video quality. Learn More About NVIDIA and Google’s Work to Build Trust SIGNS NVIDIA is partnering with the American Society for Deaf Children, Hello Monday/DEPT, and the Rochester Institute for Technology to teach and build a publicly available dataset for accessible technologies through the Signs Platform. Learn and Help Build the World’s Largest Dataset for American Sign Language Trustworthy AI in the News View All Trustworthy AI Stories Corporate Blog Technical Blog A Commitment to Research Our AI research is focused on developing algorithms and systems that can augment human capabilities, solve complex problems, and improve efficiencies across industries. We work to maintain our guiding principles of privacy, transparency, nondiscrimination, and safety and security in all research practices and methodologies. Featured publications and papers: A Safety and Security Framework for Real-World Agentic Systems Shaona Ghosh, Barnaby Simkin, Dan Zhao, Nikki Pope, Roopa Prabhu, Michael Demoret, Bartley Richardson, et al. Bias in Gender Bias Benchmarks: How Spurious Features Distort Evaluation Yusuke Hirota, Ryo Hachiuma, Boyi Li, Ximing Lu, Michael Ross Boone, Boris Ivanovic, Yejin Choi, Marco Pavone, Yu-Chiang Frank Wang, Noa Garcia, Yuta Nakashima, Chao-Han Huck Yang NVIDIA's Frontier AI Risk Assessment Barnaby Simkin, Nikki Pope, Leon Derczynski, Christopher Parisien NVIDIA Nemotron Nano 2: An Accurate and Efficient Hybrid Mamba-Transformer Reasoning Model Aarti Basant, Abhijit Khairnar, Abhijit Paithankar, Abhinav Khattar, Adithya Renduchintala, Aditya Malte, Akhiad Bercovich, Akshay Hazare, Alejandra Rico, Aleksander Ficek, Alex Kondratenko et al. FourCastNet 3: A Geometric Approach to Probabilistic Machine-Learning Weather Forecasting at Scale Boris Bonev, Thorsten Kurth, Ankur Mahesh, Mauro Bisson, Jean Kossaifi, Karthik Kashinath, Anima Anandkumar, William D. Collins, Michael S. Pritchard, Alexander Keller MambaVision: A Hybrid Mamba-Transformer Vision Backbone Ali Hatamizadeh, Jan Kautz ProtComposer: Compositional Protein Structure Generation with 3D Ellipsoids Hannes Stark, Bowen Jing, Tomas Geffner, Jason Yim, Tommi Jaakkola, Arash Vahdat, Karsten Kreis Cosmos-Reason1: From Physical Common Sense To Embodied Reasoning Alisson Azzolini, Junjie Bai, Hannah Brandon, Jiaxin Cao, Prithvijit Chattopadhyay, Huayu Chen, Jinju Chu, Yin Cui, Jenna Diamond, Yifan Ding, Liang Feng, Francesco Ferroni, Rama Govindaraju et al. GR00T N1: An Open Foundation Model for Generalist Humanoid Robots Johan Bjorck, Fernando Castañeda, Nikita Cherniadev, Xingye Da, Runyu Ding, Linxi "Jim" Fan, Yu Fang, Dieter Fox, Fengyuan Hu, Spencer Huang, Joel Jang, Zhenyu Jiang, Jan Kautz, Kaushil Kundalia, Lawrence Lao et al. Explore NVIDIA Research Discover More at the GTC AI Conference Hear from the experts and government leaders paving the way for AI regulation and trustworthiness. Explore NVIDIA GTC Trustworthy AI sessions curated to help companies identify and address potential obstacles to developing their own initiatives, including a recent discussion on Foundational Concepts in AI Safety from GTC DC. Explore GTC Sessions Company Information About Us Investors Venture Capital (NVentures) NVIDIA Foundation Research Corporate Sustainability Technologies Careers News and Events Newsroom Company Blog Technical Blog Webinars Stay Informed Events Calendar GTC AI Conference NVIDIA On-Demand Popular Links Developers Partners Executive Insights Startups and VCs NVIDIA Connect for ISVs Documentation Technical Training Professional Services for Data Science Follow NVIDIA ' title=' '> ' title=' '> NVIDIA United States Privacy Policy Your Privacy Choices Terms of Service Accessibility Corporate Policies Product Security Contact Copyright © 2026 NVIDIA Corporation","chain_hash":"c3024070d938d90148ac2c5973e8497204814f9bf87cf04e86039208020d2d21","content_hash":"ba92c3e2e64bc58c2af5f167ae6532f4f83076fd6363bdca1211cc7b94184a7a","entity_id":"commitment:nvidia:trustworthy-ai","field":"text","observed_at":"2026-07-20T20:51:24.405Z","source_url":"https://www.nvidia.com/en-us/ai-data-science/trustworthy-ai/"}
